content: Alright, I'll try to start a conversation here. How is the situation in everyone's local area?

Guess I'll start: the UK locked down this morning, but it's not a strict as in some parts of Europe. We're allowed outside once per day to exercise, and don't need signed permission or anything to go out
US has no national lockdown to my knowledge. It seems to be entirely at the will of state governments.
Germany is in „lockdown“ mode. That means, two meters distance, no gathering of more than 3 people, shops are closed, groceries are still open.

Here in VA (~40 km W of Wash DC) the state governor has closed schools, but no mandate to stay inside. Yesterday I saw lots of people packing restaurants in the nearby mall around dinnertime, fewer than 1 in 10 people I saw were wearing a mask, and most were close together.
I'm also in VA but restaurants are closed here, only delivery.

I'm in Modena, (Northern) Italy. Complete lockdown. Only essential activities are allowed to continue, and unions are announcing strikes to narrow the list of what is considered 'essential'. On the bright side, people here seem to be aware of the severity of the issue and there are few attempts at avoiding the quaratine measures (but @Fabrizio Genovese may disagree, since he lives in another part of Italy).
The situation is grim. Fortunately, if you lose yourself in math, movies and music, you can relieve the stress and take advantage of all this time alone with your thoughts. Moreover, there's plenty of silence and clean air outside to enjoy (I'm lucky enough to have a small balcony, so that I can spend some time in 'open air').
As of now, my main problems are exercise and eating well. If you have some suggestions about this, I'd appreciate.
